user_query:
We’re a married couple (Alex and Jordan) trying to strengthen our communication before considering divorce. Please create a small, reproducible “Couples Communication Practice Pack” for us by combining two evidence-based exercises from official public sources with our local preferences.

Use the inputs in:
- input/preferences.json
- input/intro.md

Internet requirement:
- Download exactly two evidence-based communication exercise PDFs from official .gov or .edu domains (no direct URLs in your deliverables).
  1) One PDF whose title includes the phrase “I-Statement” (e.g., I-Statements, I-Message, etc.).
  2) One PDF whose title includes “Active Listening”.
- Save them as:
  - downloads/resources/i_statement.pdf
  - downloads/resources/active_listening.pdf

Processing and verification (must be performed via a script you run from the command line):
1) For each PDF, compute a SHA-256 checksum and extract its plain text into a .txt file with the same base name in downloads/resources/ (e.g., downloads/resources/i_statement.txt and downloads/resources/active_listening.txt).
2) Build a manifest at downloads/resources/manifest.json with one object per resource containing exactly these fields:
   - id: "i_statement" or "active_listening"
   - downloaded_from_domain: the domain of the source (e.g., example.edu)
   - source_type: either ".gov" or ".edu"
   - original_title: a best-effort title taken from the PDF metadata or first heading/line of extracted text
   - file_name: the local PDF filename (e.g., "i_statement.pdf")
   - sha256: the checksum you computed
   - extracted_snippets: an array of at least 3 short phrases (<=140 chars each) found in the extracted text that summarize key steps or tips

Plan generation (using input/preferences.json):
- Create a 6-week plan according to schedule.weeks in input/preferences.json and save it to outputs/plan.csv with these columns:
  week, theme, exercise_title, description, source_id, at_home_prompt
  Requirements:
  - The CSV must have exactly schedule.weeks rows (from input/preferences.json).
  - theme must be one of preferences.current_challenges.
  - exercise_title must be drawn from manifest.original_title or a succinct heading found in the corresponding extracted text.
  - description must be an actionable step, up to 240 characters.
  - source_id must be either "i_statement" or "active_listening", and each source_id must appear in at least 2 different weeks.
  - at_home_prompt must address both names (“Alex” and “Jordan”) and reflect at least one item from preferences.strengths.
  - At least one row must incorporate (paraphrase or quote) one rule from preferences.communication_rules.

Guide creation:
- Create a printable guide at outputs/printable_guide.md with these sections:
  1) Intro: include the content of input/intro.md verbatim.
  2) Rules We Agree On: list all preferences.communication_rules from input/preferences.json.
  3) Weekly Plan: enumerate each row from outputs/plan.csv with a checkbox and the fields week, theme, exercise_title, and at_home_prompt.
  4) Sources & Checksums: list each resource from downloads/resources/manifest.json with original_title, downloaded_from_domain, and sha256.

Directory inspection and validation (must be done via command-line execution):
- Produce a recursive inventory of downloads/resources and outputs that includes filenames and file sizes, and save it to outputs/inventory.txt.
- Generate outputs/validation_report.txt that reports:
  - The parsed schedule.weeks value used.
  - The total number of rows in outputs/plan.csv.
  - A count of rows per source_id.
  - Whether all themes used are a subset of preferences.current_challenges (true/false).
  - Whether both source_ids appear at least twice (true/false).
  - Whether a rule from preferences.communication_rules appears in at least one row (true/false).

Constraints and notes:
- Do not hardcode external URLs; record only the domains in the manifest.
- The Internet downloads, checksum computation, text extraction, and validations must be performed by running at least one script or command-line program you provide.
- All artifacts must be reproducible by re-running your script(s).
- Deliverables must be exactly at the specified paths:
  - downloads/resources/i_statement.pdf
  - downloads/resources/active_listening.pdf
  - downloads/resources/i_statement.txt
  - downloads/resources/active_listening.txt
  - downloads/resources/manifest.json
  - outputs/plan.csv
  - outputs/printable_guide.md
  - outputs/inventory.txt
  - outputs/validation_report.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
